p1_vlm_decision.reasoning: The bed is correctly oriented when the mattress surface is horizontal and facing upward, and the headboard is vertical and at the head of the bed. G6 shows the bed in this natural upright position across all three views: the mattress is flat on top, the headboard is tall and vertical, and the bed rests stably on the ground plane. Other groups show the bed lying on its side or upside-down, with the mattress or headboard in unnatural orientations. G6 is the only group where the functional top surface (mattress) faces up and the base rests naturally on the ground.

p2_vlm_decision.reasoning: The front face of a bed is the headboard side, which is typically the taller, more decorative structure. In Angle 1, the headboard is visible on the left side of the bed, indicating a side view. In Angle 2, the headboard is fully visible and facing the camera, showing the back of the bed. In Angle 3, the headboard is on the right side, again a side view. In Angle 4, the headboard is at the top of the bed, directly facing the camera, which is the standard front view of a bed. Therefore, Angle 4 shows the front face directly facing the camera.
